ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Nov-08-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Michele McGill, via ZOOM, for Ronald Tolentino.

The court asks counsel if she has heard from Mr. Castagnola. Counsel reports that she spoke with him after the motion hearing wherein the court denied the request for relief from untimely filing of will contest and petition to revoke probate of will.

After additional discussion, the Accounting Petition is submitted.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

Will Contest And Petition To Revoke Probate Or Purported Will
Off Calendar Apr-27-2021 Off Calendar

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
Off Calendar Sep-01-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Michelle McGill, Esq., counsel for and with Ronald Tolentino, Petitioner (Ms. McGill appearing by video conferencing, Mr. Tolentino present in Court).

The Court addresses the presumption of Probate Code Sec. 6112 [beneficiary as witness] to which Ms. McGill responds, noting the various declarations supporting the Petition and the facts of the case in general. At the Court's query, Ms. McGill contends that if granted, the Petitioner should be appointed with full authority.

The Court takes the matter under submission but with the intention of granting the Petition with full authority.

Judge: Hon. Ross C. Moody; Clerk: R. Michael Diles; Reporter: Not Reported. (D204)

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
Off Calendar Jul-27-2020 Continued To Aug-24-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Michelle McGill appeared by zoom for Ronald M. Tolentino (Petitioner).

The Court noted that the issue of Probate Code 6112(c) presumption of fraud or undue influence appears to apply because her Client was one of the witnesses to the will. Ms. McGill stated that she has not received any notice of anyone inquiring or objecting, and that she can provide offer of proof, or testimony of the Petitioner, or a declaration. Ms. McGill also stated that the Decedent wished to execute a will, that the Decedent had seven Children from four different Mothers, that the Decedent was estranged from the eldest Daughter and that he did not maintain a relationship with the other Children. In addition, Ms. McGill stated that there was a divorce pending, that it was a marriage out of convenience, that at the time when the Decedent prepared the will he had been assaulted and his Mother died, and that he hired an Attorney to assist him, but the Lawyer did not get to it for over a matter of months. Furthermore, Ms. McGill stated that the Decedent signed the will in front of a Notary and his Son, that the Decedent understood what he was doing, that the Decedent did it voluntarily and told people about what he did, and that one witness is Stacie (Wife) and the other is a Cousin. The Court stated that it can go by way of declarations and that the declarations are to be served on all other parties. The Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters of Administration with Will Annexed is continued to August 24, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Court Examiner: Sandy Hilton. Deputy: C. Cabasares. Not reported.
